claims.description: red-state fixture constructs the state at the DB layer (an earlier cw2-minted grant withdrawn via that token, then a fresh tokenless grant appended after, as the CURRENT grant) — a precautionary construction per AC-1's 'constructible state' wording; no live apps/api write path today produces this sequence for the same person+org+basis (verified: processConsentResponseV2 mints a token on every approve, createDirectConsentGrant only fires for a brand-new person, appendRestoreGrant carries the token forward unchanged)
old cw2 token vs current tokenless grant -> ConsentRecordNotFoundError, current grant left un-withdrawn; red-green-revert executed
getGdprGrantWithdrawalStateV2 state-read parity returns null for the same old-cw2-vs-tokenless case
exhaustive six-row matching matrix, exercised against BOTH stampWithdrawal (write path) and getGdprGrantWithdrawalStateV2 (read path) per iteration
exact-match comment correction in both functions (removes null-as-wildcard language) + full suite green (67/67) after fix
